utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi andrea , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: doing well . uh , so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , andrea is a 52-year-old female with a past medical history significant for rheumatoid arthritis , atrial fibrillation , and reflux who presents today for her annual exam . so andrea , it's been a year since i saw you . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . so , i've been walking like you told me to and , um , exercising and doing yoga , and that's actually helped with my arthritis a lot , just the- the constant movement . so , i have n't had any joint pain recently .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . good . so , no- no issues with any stiffness or pain or flare ups over the last year ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i know that we have you on the methotrexate , are you still taking that once a week ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any issues with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no . okay . and then in terms of your a-fib , how are you doing with that ? are you having any palpitations ? or , i know that you've kind of been in and out of it over the past , you know , year or so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i've still been having palpitations . the- the last one i had was about a week ago . i've noticed that when i start to get stressed , um , they start to flare up again . so , i've been trying meditation , trying running with my dog to try and relieve the stress but it has n't really been working .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . i- i know that you had called , um , last month and we- we did that event monitor for you , uh , which we'll take a look at in a few minutes . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , how about um , your reflux ? you know , we had placed you on the protonix , uh , has that helped ? and i know that you were gon na do some dietary modifications .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i cut out soda and that- that's helped- seemed to help , and the medication's been helping too . i have n't had a flare up in over , i think , five months .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so , you know , i know that you did the review of systems sheet when you checked in and , you know , you had- you know , you endorsed the palpitations and you had some nasal congestion . any other symptoms ? you know , chest pain , shortness of breath , nausea or vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that . just the nasal- nasal congestion because of my allergies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . okay . well , i'd like to go ahead and do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey dragon , show me the vital signs . okay . so , you're in- here in the office today , it looks like , you know , your heart rate's really good today . it's- it's nice and controlled so that's good . um , i'm just gon na take a look into your heart and lungs and- and i'll let you know what i find . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , on physical examination , um , you know , everything looks really good . on your heart examination , i do appreciate a slight 2/6 systolic- systolic ejection murmur , um , which we've heard in the past so i'm not worried at that . you're in the- a nice regular rate and rhythm at this time . your lungs are nice and clear . on your right elbow , i do notice some edema and some erythema . does it hurt when i press it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does a bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , she has pain to palpation of the right elbow . um , and you have no lower extremity edema , okay ? um , so i wan na go ahead and just take a look at some of your results . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the event- event monitor results . okay . so , you know , this is the results of your event monitor which shows that , you know , you're in and out of a-fib , you have what we call a conversion pause . you know , you're in a-fib , you pause , and then you go back to regular rhythm . so , we'll talk about that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the autoimmune panel . so , looking here at your autoimmune panel , everything looks good , it looks like you're- you know , everything is well controlled with your rheumatoid arthritis on the methotrexate . okay ? so , let me just go over a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so for your first problem , your rheumatoid arthritis , again , everything looks good . i wan na just continue you on the methotrexate 2.5 mg , once weekly . um , and uh , if you need a referral back to see the rheumatologist , let me know , but i think everything seems stable now . do you need a refill of the methotrexate ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey dragon , order methotrexate , 2.5 mg once weekly . for your second problem , the atrial fibrillation . so , you're going in and out of a-fib and i'd like to just keep you in normal sinus rhythm . so , i wan na go ahead and refer you to cardiology for a cardiac ablation which just maps out where that rhythm is coming from and burns it so it does n't come back . okay ? you're young , we wan na keep you in a normal rhythm and , being that you're going in and out of a-fib , i think that's what we should do . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order a referral to cardiology . and for your last problem , the reflux , you know , i wanna- i want you to just continue on the protonix , 40 mg a day . continue with your dietary modifications , you know , avoiding coffee and spicy foods , that type of thing . okay ? and then let me know if you have any other issues with that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: will do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . it was good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good seeing you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 52-year-old female with a history of rheumatoid arthritis, atrial fibrillation, and reflux presenting for an annual exam.
Annual exam
The patient reports regular exercise and yoga have helped manage her rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced any stiffness, pain, or flare-ups of arthritis in the last year.
The patient denies any arthritis stiffness, pain, or flare-ups over the last year.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking methotrexate once a week.
The patient confirms she is taking methotrexate once a week.
The doctor asks if the patient has any issues with the methotrexate.
The patient denies any issues with methotrexate.
Atrial fibrillation
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing palpitations.
The patient reports ongoing palpitations, with the last episode occurring one week ago, triggered by stress.
The doctor acknowledges the patient had an event monitor performed last month.
Reflux
The doctor asks if the Protonix and dietary modifications have helped the patient's reflux.
The patient reports cutting out soda and taking medication has helped, with no flare-ups in over five months.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, or vomiting, but reports nasal congestion due to allergies.
The doctor declares the intent to perform a physical examination.
The doctor notes the patient's heart rate is controlled.
Physical exam findings: 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, regular heart rate and rhythm, clear lungs, and edema/erythema of the right elbow.
The doctor asks if the right elbow is painful to palpation.
The patient confirms the right elbow is painful to palpation.
The doctor notes no lower extremity edema.
The event monitor results show the patient is in and out of atrial fibrillation with a conversion pause.
The autoimmune panel is normal, indicating well-controlled rheumatoid arthritis.
The doctor plans to continue methotrexate 2.5 mg once weekly.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of methotrexate.
The patient confirms she needs a refill of methotrexate.
The doctor orders a referral to cardiology for a cardiac ablation to address atrial fibrillation.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue Protonix 40 mg daily.
The doctor advises the patient to continue dietary modifications for reflux.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
The patient has no further questions.
